Okay, let's see. The question is about putting a measured amount of liquid soap into a spray bottle. The two options given are A and B.

Option A says to use a mason jar to pour liquid soap into a spray bottle. A mason jar is typically a glass jar with a lid, used for canning or storing things. If you're pouring from a mason jar into a spray bottle, it might not help with measuring the amount unless the mason jar has measurement markings. But mason jars usually don't have those, so you might not get a precise amount.

Option B suggests using a spoon to put a little liquid soap into a spray bottle. A regular spoon also doesn't have measurement markings, so using it might not give a measured amount either. The word "little" is vague, so it's not really a measured amount.
